text.title: AWS VPC Design Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every application of `AWS VPC Design` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: VPC CIDR MUST be allocated from AWS IPAM (or equivalent); ad-hoc /16 grabs forbidden at org level.
rule.rationale: Random CIDRs cause overlaps; IPAM-managed pools prevent peering conflicts as the org grows.

rule.statement: VPC MUST have public + private + data subnets per AZ; data subnets MUST NOT have a NAT route (database isolation).
rule.rationale: Mixed-tier subnets break SG scoping + lateral-movement defence; tiered subnets are the only defensible default.

rule.statement: Production VPC MUST span ≥ 2 AZs; staging / dev MAY use 1 AZ with cost justification.
rule.rationale: Single-AZ outages are recurrent (us-east-1); Multi-AZ raises availability above 99.99%.

rule.statement: VPC MUST have Gateway endpoints for S3 + DynamoDB and Interface endpoints for STS, KMS, ECR, Secrets Manager, CloudWatch Logs, SSM, ECS.

rationale.source: AWS VPC Endpoints docs; FinOps reports

rule.rationale: NAT egress to AWS services adds cost + public-internet hop; endpoints are cheaper + private + auditable.

rule.statement: Every VPC MUST have a named human owner (handle + role) recorded in IPAM / Terraform / tags; group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anonymous VPCs become orphan-cost + orphan-risk; named owner enforces accountability.

rule.statement: New VPCs SHOULD enable IPv6 dual-stack from day 1; IPv4-only requires explicit ADR.
rule.rationale: IPv4 RFC1918 space is finite at org scale; IPv6 retrofit is harder than day-1 dual-stack.

rule.statement: VPC Flow Logs MUST be enabled at VPC level on every VPC; retention ≥ 90 days; logs in audit account.
rule.rationale: Without flow logs, post-incident forensics is impossible; VPC-level is the cheapest forensic surface.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
